2604.10417.title: LASQ: A Low-resource Aspect-based Sentiment Quadruple Extraction Dataset
2604.10417.paper_summary: In recent years, aspect-based sentiment analysis (ABSA) has made rapid progress and shown strong practical value. However, existing research and benchmarks are largely concentrated on high-resource languages, leaving fine-grained sentiment extraction in low-resource languages under-explored. To address this gap, we constructed the first Low-resource languages Aspect-based Sentiment Quadruple dataset, named LASQ, which includes two low-resource languages: Uzbek and Uyghur. Secondly, it includes a fine-grained target-aspect-opinion-sentiment quadruple extraction task. To facilitate future research, we designed a grid-tagging model that integrates syntactic knowledge. This model incorporates part-of-speech (POS) and dependency knowledge into the model through our designed Syntax Knowledge Embedding Module (SKEM), thereby alleviating the lexical sparsity problem caused by agglutinative languages. Experiments on LASQ demonstrate consistent gains over competitive baselines, validating both the dataset's utility and the effectiveness of the proposed modeling approach.
2604.10417.paper_summary_zh: 近年來，基於面向的情感分析（ABSA）取得了快速進展並顯示出強大的實際價值。<lb/>然而，現有的研究和基準主要集中在高資源語言上，使得低資源語言中的細粒度情感提取未得到充分探索。<lb/>為了解決這一問題，我們構建了第一個低資源語言基於面向的情感四元組數據集，命名為LASQ，其中包括兩種低資源語言：烏茲別克語和維吾爾語。<lb/>其次，它包括一個細粒度的目標-面向-意見-情感四元組提取任務。<lb/>為了促進未來的研究，我們設計了一個整合語法知識的網格標記模型。<lb/>該模型通過我們設計的語法知識嵌入模塊（SKEM）將詞性（POS）和依賴知識納入模型中，從而緩解了由於粘著語言引起的詞彙稀疏問題。<lb/>在LASQ上的實驗顯示出相對於競爭基準的一致增益，驗證了數據集的實用性和所提建模方法的有效性。
2604.10417.author: Aizihaierjiang Yusufu et.al.
2604.10417.authors: Aizihaierjiang Yusufu, Jiang Liu, Kamran Aziz, Abidan Ainiwaer, Bobo Li, Fei Li, Donghong Ji, Aizierguli Yusufu
